agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to explore funding for student scholarships
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a presentation from Richmond Promise, a local organization that helps Richmond students access and complete post-secondary education (including college, trade school, and university). After the presentation, Council will consider directing staff to explore funding options to support scholarships for Richmond youth. This is a proposed step — no funding has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision on funding exploration.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Presentation from Richmond Promise: Helping to build a stronger Richmond by deeply supporting Richmond students to access and successfully complete post-secondary education (not only 4 year college)
•  RECEIVE a presentation from Richmond Promise on the organization’s current scope of services, how it supports youth before, during, and after post-secondary education, and the impact it has achieved to date and to highlight the program’s current needs as it continues to serve Richmond students; and DIRECT staff to explore funding to support scholarships for Richmond youth - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on opposing CO2 pipeline project
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve injecting carbon dioxide waste underground and building a network of pipelines through Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez has proposed the resolution, citing risks to the health and safety of Richmond residents. The item is up for public comment and council deliberation — it has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit public comment to voice your concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting. THE PRESENTATION FOR THIS ITEM WAS RECEIVED ON MAY 5, 2026. THIS ITEM IS FOR PUBLIC COMMENT AND COUNCIL DELIBERATION ONLY.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to hold public hearing on city vacancies and recruitment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider holding a public hearing to receive a presentation on the status of city vacancies and the city's recruitment and retention efforts in 2025. This is a proposed item on the agenda — it has not yet been approved or enacted. The hearing would allow the public to hear about how the city is filling empty positions and keeping staff.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to learn about city hiring and share your input.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.4
Annual Public Hearing on Vacancies
•  HOLD a public hearing and RECEIVE a presentation on the status of vacancies and the City’s recruitment and retention efforts in 2025 – Human Resources (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6803).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on new Master Fee Schedule
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold a public hearing and introduce a proposed ordinance (a local law) that would update the Master Fee Schedule. This proposed update would change the fees the city charges for various permits, licences, and services. The first reading of the ordinance is up for approval at the next council meeting. If passed, these new fees could affect the cost of doing business in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support for the proposed fee changes.

key_dates.label: Richmond Master Fee Schedule update — public hearing

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.3
Master Fee Schedule Update
•  HOLD a public hearing; and INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) establishing certain fees for the proposed Master Fee Schedule – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to levy special assessments for unpaid fines and cleanup costs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to add unpaid administrative citation fees and nuisance abatement costs directly onto property tax bills as a special assessment. This means if a property owner does not pay fines for things like code violations or city-ordered cleanup, the city could collect the money through the property tax system. A public hearing will be held before any decision is made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support before council votes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on special assessments for unpaid fines — Richmond

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.2
Levy of Special Assessments for Unpaid Administrative Citation and Nuisance Abatement Costs
•  HOLD a public hearing; and ADOPT a resolution authorizing the levy of special assessments against certain properties with unpaid invoices related to administrative citation fees, and nuisance abatement costs pursuant to Richmond Municipal Code Chapters 2.62, 6.38, 9.22, and 11.76 – Community Development Department/Code Enforcement Division (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1279).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond zoning and land-use changes to match state housing law
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the city's zoning rules and General Plan (the city's long-term land-use blueprint). These changes are meant to bring Richmond's rules in line with recent updates to state housing law, as part of the city's 6th Cycle Housing Element. If approved, the amendments would affect several sections of the Richmond Municipal Code (the city's local laws) and the General Plan's Land Use and Urban Design Element. A public hearing will be held before the vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns about the proposed zoning and land-use changes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on proposed Richmond zoning amendments

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
General Plan and Zoning Text Amendments Related to 6th Cycle Housing Element Program Implementation
•  HOLD a public hearing; INTRODUCE (first reading) an ordinance, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending Articles 15.04.104, 15.04.201, 15.04.202, 15.04.203, 15.04.602, 15.04.607; Sections 15.04.610.180, 15.04.610.360, 15.04.805.040 and 15.04.806.040 of the Richmond Municipal Code (RMC); and ADOPT a resolution, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element, to implement certain programs identified in the City’s adopted 6th Cycle Housing Element to align with recent changes to State Law - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft budget and capital plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. As part of this review, council will receive updates from the Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments. This is a proposed budget — it has not been approved yet. The final decision will come after all departmental presentations are complete and council discusses the plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this proposed budget could affect business licence fees and city services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Fiscal Year 2026-27 Draft Budget Department Presentations and Budget Updates
•  RECEIVE an update on departmental presentations from Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments as part of the continued review of the draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 FiveYear Capital Improvement Plan, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations; and RECEIVE updated financial and operational considerations – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on sidewalk vendor rule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider proposed changes to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (Richmond Municipal Code Chapter 7.42). The proposed bylaw would update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms for sidewalk vendors. This item was previously discussed at meetings on April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, and is now up for a first reading (an initial vote to introduce the ordinance). If approved, it would change how vendors apply for permits, how waivers are handled, and how rules are enforced. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, it could affect your sidewalk vending licence or application process.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.f
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $390,000 electric bikeshare contract amendment up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility for an additional $390,000, bringing the total contract to $3,540,341. This would keep the citywide electric bikeshare program running until the end of the fiscal year while council decides the program's long-term future during the 2026-27 budget process. The money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This item was previously postponed from meetings on April 28 and May 5, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect transportation options for your customers and employees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.e
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).​ This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $1M for Santa Fe Avenue slope fix
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposal to fund the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project. The plan would take $1,000,000 from the city's Insurance Reserve Fund to pay for construction. It would also move $72,623.62 from another project (Santa Rita Road Stabilization) and $205,502.81 from a state-funded Quick-Build project to cover design and geotechnical work. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. If passed, the money would be added to the 2026–27 budget for the slope repair work.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this could affect nearby properties and traffic on Santa Fe Avenue.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.d
Appropriate Funds for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to RATIFY the transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project; APPROPRIATE $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project funded through California’s Active Transportation Program for Cutting/Hoffman Project to fund design and geotechnical services; and APPROPRIATE $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund to the General Capital Fund in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 budget for construction of the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/ Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $3.25M road repair fund list
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would approve a list of road maintenance and rehabilitation projects for Fiscal Year 2026–27. If passed, the resolution would also appropriate $3,251,983 from the Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) — a state fund for fixing roads — into the city's Public Works Department budget. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.c
Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Fund Project List
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and APPROPRIATE $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to SB 1 Fund 1019 - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Robert Armijo 510-620-5477).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on Complete Streets funding plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed funding allocation plan for Complete Streets Improvements. This plan would direct money toward pavement management, streetscape upgrades, and other street improvements across Richmond. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting. If approved, the plan would affect how public works funds are spent on local roads and sidewalks.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect street construction and access near your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.b
Complete Streets Improvement Elements Project Funding Allocation Plan
•  APPROVE the Complete Streets Improvements (Pavement Management, Streetscape Improvements, etc.) funding allocation plan – Public Works Department (Josef Munoz 510-620-6671/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to accept Lincoln Elementary Safe Routes project as complete
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution to formally accept the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete. If approved, this would authorize the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion (a formal document stating the project is finished) and authorize the city engineer to release the bonds and retention money held as security during construction. This is a routine step to close out the project, not a new rule or regulation.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.a
Acceptance of the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete; AUTHORIZE the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion; and AUTHORIZE the city engineer to execute and release applicable bonds and retention – Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department worth up to $7.2 million for ongoing animal control services. The proposed agreement covers Fiscal Year 2026–2027 and includes an option to extend for three more years (2027–2030). This item was postponed from the May 5, 2026 meeting and is now up for a vote. If approved, the contract would run from July 1, 2022 through June 30, 2030. The Police Department is recommending approval.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract could affect animal control fees or services for your business.

key_dates.label: Council vote on animal control contract — Richmond

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.c
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
•  APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802). This item was continued from the May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on 3-year shooting range rental deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year, sole-source contract with the Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals. A sole-source contract means the city would negotiate directly with this one club, without opening the deal to other bidders. The proposed contract would cost up to $25,000 per year (total up to $75,000) from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, with two optional one-year extensions at $25,000 each. The Police Department is recommending this deal. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this contract could affect local shooting range access and police training costs.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — Richmond Rod and Gun Club rental deal
Proposed contract end date — Richmond Rod and Gun Club rental deal

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.b
Contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for Shooting Range Facility Rental
•  APPROVE a three-year sole-source contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals in an amount not to exceed $25,000 annually, for a total not to exceed $75,000, for the term July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two one-year extension options at $25,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $150K GPS contract for police vehicles
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Verizon Connect to provide GPS tracking and management for police vehicles. If approved, the three-year contract would cost up to $150,000 starting July 1, 2026, with options to extend for two additional years at up to $50,000 per year. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.

key_dates.label: Proposed GPS contract start date — Richmond Police
Proposed GPS contract end date — Richmond Police

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.a
Contract with Verizon Connect for Police Vehicle GPS Service
•  APPROVE a contract with Verizon Connect for monthly GPS tracking and management in an amount not to exceed $150,000 over a three-year term from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, plus two one-year extension options not to exceed $50,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Four new stakeholder seats added to Macdonald Avenue Task Force
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on adding four new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force. If approved, these seats would be filled through the standard boards and commissions process. The Mayor's Office (Eduardo Martinez) is the contact for this proposal. This is not yet a done deal — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact the Mayor's Office (Eduardo Martinez, 510-620-6502) to express interest or get details on the appointment process.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.11.e
Expansion of the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force Membership
•  APPROVE the addition of four (4) new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force which will be filled through the standard boards and commissions process—Mayor’s Office (Eduardo Martinez (510-620-6502).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council committee to rewrite meeting rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to create a special committee (called an 'ad-hoc committee') that would be tasked with rewriting the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order. The goal is to make council meetings run more smoothly and efficiently. If approved, the mayor would appoint members to this committee, and they would have 60 days to propose changes for the full council to vote on. This is a proposed step — no rules have been changed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for the council decision on whether to create this committee.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.10.a
Ad-Hoc Committee to Amend City Council Rules of Procedure and Order
•  REQUEST that Mayor Eduardo Martinez appoint a City Council ad hoc committee to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager to develop proposed amendments to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order and other operational refinements to improve efficiency, for consideration by the full City Council within 60 days of the committee’s appointment - City Clerk’s Office/City Attorney’s Office/City Manager’s Office (Pamela Christian 510-620-6513/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509/Shasa Curl 510-620-6512).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 5% salary adjustment for Richmond city clerk in 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a 5% salary increase for the city clerk, effective March 2, 2026. This proposed adjustment is part of the executive management agreement with the union IFPTE Local 21. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed city clerk salary adjustment takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.9.a
Annual Compensation Adjustment for the City Clerk
•  APPROVE a five percent (5%) salary adjustment for the city clerk effective March 2, 2026, in accordance with the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding Section 6.4D – Human Resources Department (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6602).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on wildfire preparedness committee recommendations
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider recommendations from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee to improve the city's wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts. Council will provide direction to staff on which recommendation to pursue during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations. The committee will then be disbanded. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could affect your property or business if new wildfire rules or fees are proposed.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.c
Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee Recommendations
•  RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on EMS agreement with Contra Costa County
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the existing interagency agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for the Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Program. If approved, the amendment would extend the agreement through June 30, 2027, and add $229,462 in Measure H funding, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is a proposed administrative agreement between the city and county.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.b
Interagency Agreement Amendment with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program
•  ADOPT a resolution to: (1) APPROVE and AUTHORIZE the city manager, or designee to execute an amendment to the agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program, extending the term of the current interagency agreement through June 30, 2027; and (2) ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE funds in an amount not to exceed an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding from the Contra Costa County Health Services, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Deputy Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to designate officials for disaster aid applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to designate specific officials who would be authorized to apply for financial disaster assistance from the California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services and the federal government under the Stafford Act. This is a proposed step to prepare for future disaster relief — it has not been approved yet. If passed, it would allow the Fire Department to quickly submit applications for state and federal funds after a disaster.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.a
Designating Officials Authorized to Execute and Submit Application for Federal and State Financial Disaster Assistance
•  ADOPT a resolution designating officials that are authorized to execute and submit applications to the State of California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for state financial assistance under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act – Fire Department (Shasa Curl 510-620-6512/Emily Combs 510-620-6740/Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond City Donation Acceptance Policy up for approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to approve a new Donation Acceptance Policy for the City of Richmond. This policy would set rules for how the city can accept donations from individuals, businesses, or organizations. If approved, it would create a clear process for city staff to review and accept or decline donations. The policy is meant to ensure donations are handled transparently and ethically. The Finance Department is recommending council adopt this policy at the upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.c
Donation Acceptance Policy
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Jerry Chak 510-620-6569).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond pension tax override rate set at 0.14% for 2026-27
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would set the tax rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund at 0.14% for the 2026-27 fiscal year. This is the first reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced but not yet approved. If passed, this rate would apply to property taxes to fund pension obligations for city employees. The Finance Department is recommending this rate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this proposed tax rate — it could affect your property taxes.

key_dates.label: First reading of pension tax override ordinance — Richmond Council

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.b
Setting Tax Rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-27
•  INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) setting the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-2027 at 0.14 percent – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/ Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to write off $535,499 in uncollected debts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to write off $535,499.12 in unpaid accounts receivable. 'Write-off' means the city would officially give up trying to collect these debts and remove them from its books. This does not cancel the debt — the city could still try to collect in the future — but it is a signal that the city considers these amounts unlikely to be paid. The proposal covers delinquent (overdue) and uncollectible accounts across various city services. A decision is expected at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — it may signal tighter future collection practices.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.a
Approval of Write-Off of Uncollectible Accounts Receivables
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the write-off of delinquent and/or uncollectible accounts receivable in the amount of $535,499.12 – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $60,000 boost for Richmond small business facade program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a $60,000 increase to a contract with the Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center. This would bring the total contract to $400,000 and extend it through June 30, 2027. The money supports the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program, which helps local businesses pay for building front repairs and upgrades. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this program could help fund your storefront improvements.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.b
Contract Amendment with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center for Small Business Beautification Pilot Program
•  APPROVE the first amendment to the contract with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center to increase the total contract amount by $60,000 for a revised amount not to exceed $400,000, extend the term through June 30, 2027, and support payment administration for eligible businesses participating in the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program - Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Downtown Richmond property assessment levy for FY 2026-27
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to levy assessments on property owners in the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District for the 2026-27 fiscal year. If approved, these charges would be added to property tax bills through Contra Costa County. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been voted on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this levy will appear on your property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District Fiscal Year 2026–2027 Assessment Levy
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the City of Richmond to levy assessments on property owners subject to the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District through the County of Contra Costa property tax rolls for Fiscal Year 2026–2027 – Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to accept $35,000 Chevron grant for street outreach
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider accepting a $35,000 grant from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation. If approved, the money would go to the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to fund street outreach and mobile crisis response work. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time. Watch for the council vote — this grant does not directly affect business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Grant Award from Chevron Community Engagement Foundation
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE a $35,000 grant award from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation for the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to Enhance Street Outreach/Mobile Crises Response – Community Services Department (LaShonda White/Sam Vaughn 510-620-5404).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $50,000 housing program contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year, on-call contract with Makanai, LLC for up to $50,000. The contract would provide budget and reporting support for past housing programs. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Contract with Makanai, LLC for Budget and Reporting Support for Housing Programs
•  APPROVE an on-call contract with Makanai, LLC to provide budget and reporting support related to past housing programs in an amount not to exceed $50,000, for a three-year term with a term expiration of June 30, 2029, with one one-year extension option – Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Jesus Morales 510-620-6706).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K hearing officer contracts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to award on-call contracts to two law firms — Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law — to serve as Administrative Hearing Officers. These officers act like independent judges who hear disputes about local bylaws (for example, property or business licence issues). If approved, each firm could be paid up to $300,000 over a three-year term. The decision has not been made yet; council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these contracts could affect how your business licence or property disputes are handled.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a
On-Call Contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law for Administrative Hearing Officer Services
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing on-call contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law, to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services, for an amount not to exceed $300,000 per firm, for a three-year term - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1283).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on fee waiver for WCCUSD Latino/x graduation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to waive all fees and staffing costs for the West Contra Costa Unified School District's Latino/x Graduation Ceremony at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026. If approved, the city manager would be authorized to spend city resources — including overtime for police, fire, public works, and other departments — to support the event. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been voted on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565) to express support or concerns before the council vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Waiver of Auditorium Fees, Staffing Costs, and Other Associated Costs to Support the West Contra Costa Unified School District Latino/x Graduation at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium and Convention Center
•  REQUEST the city manager issue fee waivers to West Contra Costa Unified School District for the use of the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026 for the Latino/x Graduation Ceremony or Ceremonies, and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to expend City resources to support the graduation, which may include overtime from Police, Fire, Public Works, Community Services, and other departments as required  - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $110,000 legal research contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services. If approved, the contract would provide the City Attorney's Office with access to Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal for three years, starting July 1, 2026, at a cost not to exceed $110,000. This is a routine operational expense for the city's legal team and does not directly affect business owners or the public.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Contract with Thomson Reuters Enterprise for Legal Research Subscription Services
•  APPROVE a contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services for Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal in an amount not to exceed $110,000 for the term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, subject to non-substantive revisions approved by the City Attorney’s Office; and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the contract – City Attorney’s Office (Christopher Dykzeul 510-620-6507/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $150K personnel investigation contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for the City of Richmond. The proposed contract would run from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, and would not exceed $150,000 total. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Ellis Investigations for Confidential Personnel Investigations
•  APPROVE a legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for a term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 – City Attorney’s Office/Human Resources Department (Shannon Moore/Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $75,000 increase for Winehaven legal fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with the law firm Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC. If approved, this would increase the total amount the city can spend on legal fees for the Winehaven Legacy lawsuit by $75,000, bringing the total contract to $450,000. The agreement would also be extended through December 31, 2027. The extra $75,000 would come from the city's General Fund (the main pool of taxpayer money used for everyday city services). This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a
Legal Services Agreement No. 4 with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC
•  APPROVE a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal representation related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters, to increase compensation by $75,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $450,000, extend the term through December 31, 2027, and APPROPRIATE $75,000 from the General Fund to cover associated legal fees. – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).
